name: Tébar — circular desde Cañada Juncosa
region_intro: Touring cycling around Atalaya Del Cañavate offers routes across expansive plains and undulating hills in the Mancha Alta region of Cuenca, Spain. The area features varied topography, including the impressive Hoces de Alarcón gorge and the significant Embalse de Alarcón reservoir on the Júcar River. The municipal area is characterized by a plain interspersed with hills, with the highest point being the Pico del Mojón Blanco at 896 meters. Geological features include reddish clays and whiter limestones, providing diverse backdrops for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Atalaya Del Cañavate The most popular touring cycling route is Return to the Álarcón reservoir from Honrubia, a 57.8 miles (93.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 52 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers extensive views around the large reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ermita de Pozo Amargo loop from Sisante, a difficult 26.3 miles (42.3 km) path. This route explores the countryside around Sisante, a village known for its picturesque atmosphere. Local touring cyclists also love the Tébar — circular desde Cañada Juncosa, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail leading through the plains and gentle hills of the region, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Touring cycling around Atalaya Del Cañavate is defined by expansive plains, undulating hills, and routes along the Júcar River and Embalse de Alarcón. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Atalaya Del Cañavate are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Atalaya Del Cañavate's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Atalaya Del Cañavate?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;10 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Atalaya Del Cañavate, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Atalaya Del Cañavate?
A: Touring cycling around Atalaya Del Cañavate offers a diverse landscape, moving from expansive plains to undulating hills. You'll encounter geological features like reddish clays and whiter limestones, and routes often feature views of the Júcar River and the significant Embalse de Alarcón reservoir. The region also includes impressive natural features like the Hoces de Alarcón gorge.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, for those looking for an easier ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094033772/tebar-circular-desde-canada-juncosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tébar — circular desde Cañada Juncosa&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option. This 14.3-mile (23.0 km) trail leads through plains and gentle hills, typically completed in under two hours, making it ideal for a relaxed cycling experience.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357793943/return-to-the-alarcon-reservoir-from-honrubia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Álarcón reservoir from Honrubia&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 57.8-mile (93.0 km) route with significant elevation gain, offering extensive views around the large reservoir. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31870092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Pozo Amargo loop from Sisante&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the countryside around the picturesque village of Sisante.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in historical and natural attractions. You can explore the charming village of Sisante, recognized as the most beautiful village in Castilla-La Mancha, with its notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5319159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall and Main Square&lt;/a&gt;. The impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces de Alarcón gorge&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views, and the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4157133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a stunning focal point for longer routes. Additionally, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5769666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen de Trascastillo&lt;/a&gt; in El Cañavate.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Atalaya Del Cañavate?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094033772/tebar-circular-desde-canada-juncosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tébar — circular desde Cañada Juncosa&lt;/a&gt;, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20137617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Juncosa – El Calvario loop from Cañada Juncosa&lt;/a&gt;, and the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31870092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Pozo Amargo loop from Sisante&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Atalaya Del Cañavate?
A: The region's varied topography and the presence of shaded areas along routes like the Hoces de Alarcón make it suitable for cycling across various seasons. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the cooler temperatures of winter.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling options in the area?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, routes with lower difficulty and shorter distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094033772/tebar-circular-desde-canada-juncosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tébar — circular desde Cañada Juncosa&lt;/a&gt;, are generally more suitable for families looking for a relaxed ride. These routes often traverse plains and gentle hills, providing a less strenuous experience.

Q: What is the terrain like on the touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain around Atalaya Del Cañavate is quite varied. You'll find routes crossing expansive plains, offering long, relatively flat sections. Other routes venture into undulating hills, providing more elevation changes and panoramic views. The highest point in the municipal area is the Pico del Mojón Blanco at 896 meters, indicating some significant climbs are possible on certain trails.

Q: What do other cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Atalaya Del Cañavate?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from expansive plains to undulating hills, and the scenic beauty of the Júcar River and the Embalse de Alarcón. The blend of natural landscapes and historical villages like Sisante also adds to the appeal for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near historical villages?
A: Yes, several routes offer the chance to explore historical villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31870092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Pozo Amargo loop from Sisante&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the countryside surrounding Sisante, a village celebrated for its picturesque atmosphere and historical architecture. The nearby village of Alarcón is also renowned for its medieval heritage and impressive castle, which can be a point of interest on longer routes.

Q: What is the average duration for a touring cycling route in this region?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Atalaya Del Cañavate varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, an easy route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094033772/tebar-circular-desde-canada-juncosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tébar — circular desde Cañada Juncosa&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes, while a challenging route such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357793943/return-to-the-alarcon-reservoir-from-honrubia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Álarcón reservoir from Honrubia&lt;/a&gt; can take around 5 hours 52 minutes.
tips: A small municipality in Cuenca with low, white houses. The most important point is the parish church of the Assumption from the 16th century. If you have more time for a walk, 3 km to the northeast of this town is the Pino Lorito area, a hundred-year-old tree with a 40-meter crown, one of the most impressive bicentennial pines in Castilla-La Mancha.

A small municipality in Cuenca where you can visit different monuments such as the parish church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción or the Hermitage of San Roque, and the Lavajillo lagoon (a few meters north of the town), where there is a picnic area .
